mnemonics: Static Helpers Are Interface Tools (SHAIT) - Static methods are Helper utilities Added to Interfaces as Tools.
keyPoints: Static methods in interfaces are associated with the interface itself, not with any instance of an implementing class
They are defined using the 'static' keyword and must have a body
Static interface methods are primarily used to provide utility methods related to the interface's purpose
commonMistakes: Trying to override a static method in an implementing class (static methods are not inherited)
Attempting to call a static interface method using an instance of a class rather than the interface name
Forgetting to provide a method body for a static method in an interface
